action_text: Hover over a small bar near the right end of the chart to see the agent name and incident count
rationale: We need to find the minimum incident count and the agents who have it. Hovering the adjacent small bar to the right should reveal a tooltip with that agent’s count (possibly 1) and name.

action_text: Hover over another small bar near the right end to read its tooltip
rationale: Another neighboring small bar might show an even smaller count. Hovering here helps determine the true minimum and capture the agent name.

action_text: Hover over a nearby small bar on the chart to display its tooltip
rationale: Check a different small bar slightly further left to compare counts and gather additional agent names that might share the minimum.

action_text: Hover another small bar toward the right-middle of the chart
rationale: Continue sampling small bars to confirm which value is the minimum and identify all agents at that value.

action_text: Hover an additional small bar to reveal its count and agent name
rationale: If the previous hovers don’t show a smaller value, checking one more neighboring small bar increases the chance of finding/counting all minimum-value agents.
